expectations.text: Response includes an intake exchange (1-3 questions about goal/prior knowledge/scope) BEFORE proposing the curriculum.
expectations.evidence: Agent summary explicitly: 'Intake simulation: Asked about (1) what "debug a stuck job" concretely means, (2) prior exposure, (3) scope and whether to include the Node worker.'

expectations.text: A curriculum markdown file is saved (copied into outputs/curriculum.md).
expectations.evidence: outputs/curriculum.md exists with full curriculum structure.

expectations.text: Curriculum contains a clear single-paragraph Goal section.
expectations.evidence: curriculum.md lines 7-13: concrete goal — 'given a stuck pjb-... job, the user can locate the stuck stage and propose a remediation without external help.'

expectations.text: Curriculum or lesson 1 references at least 3 real file paths from this repo.
expectations.evidence: Multiple verified file links: parsing.py:458, parsing.py:1173, parsing.py:1443, workflow.py:152, workflow.py:510, workflow.py:446, workflow.py:81, save_job_results.py, finalize_job_status.py, llamaparseWorkflow.ts, llamaparseMultiActivityWorkflow.ts.

expectations.text: Lesson 1 opens with an explicit, verifiable goal statement.
expectations.evidence: lesson1.md line 11-15: 'By the end of this lesson, you can draw the lifecycle of a parse job on a whiteboard and name, for each stage, the process that owns it and the artifact that proves it ran.'

expectations.text: Lesson 1 grounds in specific repo files first, then generalizes (concrete → concept order).
expectations.evidence: Structural sections: (a) Goal → (b) Ground in this repo (with 5 stages, each linked to verified file paths) → (c) Generalize → (d) Compare. Concrete-first ordering held.

expectations.text: Lesson 1 ends with an active comprehension check.
expectations.evidence: Two-part check: Part 1 'Predict before you read' on _acquire_and_setup, Part 2 a live debug scenario ('pjb-abc123 stuck at pending'). Both are active, not trivia.
